content_markdown:
The diagram below represents the fundamental (first harmonic) standing wave of sound inside a pipe.

Which of the following correctly represents the displacement of the air at P and Q?

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a pipe closed at one end and open at the other. Inside, the fundamental standing wave envelope is shown with a node at the closed end and an antinode at the open end. Two points, P and Q, are marked inside the pipe, with Q being closer to the open end than P.

options.label: A
options.text: P: short horizontal double-headed arrow; Q: long horizontal double-headed arrow

options.label: B
options.text: P: long horizontal double-headed arrow; Q: long horizontal double-headed arrow

options.label: C
options.text: P: short vertical double-headed arrow; Q: long vertical double-headed arrow

options.label: D
options.text: P: long vertical double-headed arrow; Q: long vertical double-headed arrow

vibe_explanation: Sound waves in a pipe are longitudinal, meaning the air molecules oscillate parallel to the direction of wave propagation (horizontally in this diagram). In a standing wave, the amplitude of displacement increases from the node (closed end) to the antinode (open end). Since Q is closer to the open end than P, it will have a larger horizontal displacement amplitude than P.
